title: Lift Lobby
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:The Lift Lobby}}
'''TERMINAL'''
The Lift Lobby
'''FREQUENCY'''
Seldom
'''LOCATION'''
Man-made structures, [[The Frontrooms]]
'''DESTINATION'''
Many levels, [[The Backrooms]]
'''DESCRIPTION'''
A transitory space between Reality and Wake.
{{Blurimage|file=lift-lobby.jpg|caption=An image of the Lift Lobby in a Frontrooms airport. Photographer did not dare approach nearer.|hovertext=Transition...}} == I — Overview == The '''Lift Lobby''' is a terminal which functions as a transitional limbo between [[the Frontrooms]] and [[the Backrooms]]. It appears rarely in man-made structures around the world, taking the place of an ordinary elevator lobby for the estimated duration of three hours before moving to a new location; however, the entry of wanderers into the Lift Lobby can also trigger a change in its position. This terminal is most likely to take the form of more "liminal" elevator lobbies, namely those which resemble commercial back-of-house areas wherein decorations are scarce and an atmosphere of overall crudeness (as compared to the main building) is present; it apparently cannot possess areas which are not segregated rooms within their buildings. When an individual or group of individuals accesses the '''Lift Lobby''', any doors or openings that connect it to the outside world are reported to instantly seal up, turning into solid, indestructible wall. Any individual present will experience a dreamy, tranquil sensation, calming their nerves from any immediate panic, yet still allowing a creeping sense of vague dread to permeate through their mind. From this point onward, the Lift Lobby is considered to be gravitating between dimensions and establishing a wireless connection either [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internet to the Frontrooms] or [[Phenomenon 2|to the Backrooms]] is impossible; no-clipping presumably results in the wanderer emerging in [[the Void]] or [[Uncharted Territory]]. Upon entry to the '''Lift Lobby''', an elevator will typically pull up within five minutes, regardless of whether the wanderer has called it by pressing the button. The interior of this elevator resembles the Frontrooms counterpart which it is mimicking, except without any buttons or method of input present whatsoever; the doors to the elevator will stay tantalisingly open until a wanderer steps in, upon which they will snap closed and the lift will begin its descent. If there are any individuals remaining in the Lift Lobby, the elevator will return for them — interestingly, individuals can only enter the elevator one at a time, as an anomalous force seemingly prevents the concurrent entry of multiple people. The lift itself is known to give an impression of descent after the doors are closed, and as a result several amateurs have attempted to use this as evidence that the Backrooms lies "beneath" [[the Frontrooms]], although most experts agree that this sensation is purely illusionary and that the elevator is actually travelling interdimensionally, along the axes of a higher dimension. It acts as a vessel by which wanderers are transported from the '''Lift Lobby''' into the Backrooms proper; as with how the terminal manifests in the Frontrooms, the lift takes the place of an arbitrarily selected [[Object 34|elevator]] in the level at which it alights. There appears to be only a singular iteration of the '''Lift Lobby''', as no two instances have been reported to exist simultaneously. However, due to lack of research on this phenomenon, the existence of multiple Lift Lobbies is entirely within the realm of possibility. == II — Gates == Based on multiple accounts, the destination of the elevator in the '''Lift Lobby''' appears to be thematically dependent on the type of structure from which the terminal is accessed in [[the Frontrooms]]. Below is a table of common ingresses and the levels in [[the Backrooms]] which they are speculated to link to — note that many of these entries are based on calculated estimations and should not be taken as gospel. {| class="fandom-table" | '''Ingress''' | '''Destination(s)''' |- | Airports | [[Level 36]], [[Level 158]], [[Level 884]] |- | Galleries, museums | [[Level 29]], [[Level 195]], [[Level 918]], [[The Mind Museum]] |- | Hospitals | [[Level 14]], [[Phenomenon 354]] |- | Hotels, resorts | [[Level 5]], [[Level 19]], [[Level 66]], [[Level 188]], [[Level 330]] |- | Mixed-use complexes | [[Level 0]], [[The Corrupted Instability]], [[Absolute Infinity of Unknown|The Absolute Infinity of the Unknown]] |- | Office complexes | [[Level 4]], [[Level 157]] |- | Parking garages | [[Level 1]], [[Level 22]], [[Level 194]], [[The Hub]] |- | Residential complexes, condominiums | [[Level 93]], [[Level 130|Level 13?]] |- | Shopping centres, malls | [[Level 33]], [[Level 59]], [[Level 121]], [[Level 296]] |- | Theatres, cinemas | [[Level 444]], [[Level 850]], [[MOTION PICTURE]] |- | Train stations | [[Level 154]], [[The Metro]], [[The Death Locomotive|The Subway]] |- | Warehouses, factories | [[Level 2]], [[Level 3]], [[Level 20]] |- |} == III — Discovery and Documentation == The first known account of the '''Lift Lobby''' stems a journal uncovered in [[Level 5]] which seems to date from the early 1940s, describing an American businessman's unknowing entry into the terminal while staying at the Waldorf Astoria hotel in New York City, USA. The phenomenon was first properly documented, under the designation of a [[:Category:Terminals|terminal]], during 2004 in parascientific journal ''Phenomenal'', then curated by the [[M.E.G.|Major Explorer Group]]. At present, the Lift Lobby is not considered a major focal point amongst any research group, and information on the terminal is still relatively lacking. Albeit, the [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organisation]] has proposed that the actual and metaphysical effects of the Lift Lobby may theoretically be reversible, and that the elevator could hypothetically be called down from the terminal and used to send wanderers back up; it is unsure whether this method could bring individuals back to [[the Frontrooms]]. ---- {{Collapse|initial='''+ Open Author Credits'''|final='''- Close Author Credits'''|body='''Author & Photographer:''' [[User:The Bishop in the Blue Dimension|Bishop]]
